stories.headline: Spring snowstorm brings freeze warning, hundreds of flight delays to Denver metro
stories.summary:
A spring storm swept through the Denver metro area Friday, producing snow showers and dropping temperatures to dangerous lows. The National Weather Service issued a Freeze Warning from 8 p.m. Friday through 8 a.m. Saturday for the Front Range urban corridor and northeast plains, with overnight lows expected to plunge to between 18 and 23 degrees — potentially damaging fruit trees, sensitive vegetation and above-ground irrigation lines.

Accumulation in Denver itself was limited to less than an inch, as warm road surfaces prevented significant sticking. Surrounding areas fared differently, with up to 4 to 6 inches possible in Evergreen and 2 to 3 inches in Castle Rock. The National Weather Service warned of travel impacts in the mountains, southern foothills and higher elevations along I-25 near the Palmer Divide.

The storm caused significant disruptions at Denver International Airport, where more than 800 flights were delayed or canceled by Friday evening. FlightAware reported 794 delays and 30 cancellations, with Southwest Airlines leading delays at 242, followed by United Airlines at 225 and SkyWest at 189. Deicing operations at DIA wrapped up before 5 p.m.

Residents were advised to disconnect water hoses, drain irrigation lines and protect tender plants from the cold. Temperatures are forecast to recover through the weekend, with sunny skies and milder conditions expected by Saturday afternoon.

stories.headline: Fatal shooting reported near Forest Street and East Thrill Place in Denver
stories.summary:
A person who was shot overnight in Denver died from their injuries Friday morning, police said. Denver Police first reported the shooting around 3:45 a.m. and later confirmed the incident occurred near the intersection of Forest Street and East Thrill Place, just north of Martin Luther King Boulevard.

The victim transported themselves to a hospital after the shooting before succumbing to their wounds. Police are investigating the death as a homicide.

Authorities have not released information about the circumstances of the shooting or identified any suspects. The case remains under active investigation.

stories.headline: Pedestrian killed by vehicle in early morning Arapahoe County crash
stories.summary:
A pedestrian was struck and killed by a car early Friday morning in Arapahoe County near the Foxfield area. The crash occurred around 2 a.m. at the intersection of East Arapahoe Road and South Lewiston Way, according to the Arapahoe County Sheriff's Office and South Metro Fire Rescue.

The collision closed all eastbound lanes of Arapahoe Road between South Parker Road and South Lewiston Way for several hours as investigators worked the scene. The lanes were fully reopened by approximately 5:15 a.m.

Authorities did not identify the victim or provide details about how the crash occurred. The investigation is ongoing.

stories.headline: Semitrailer partially rolls off I-70 bridge in west metro Denver, snarling traffic
stories.summary:
A semitrailer crashed and partially rolled off a bridge on eastbound Interstate 70 in west metro Denver on Friday afternoon, closing two lanes and backing up traffic for miles. The crash occurred at approximately 2:34 p.m. near the Colfax Avenue area, according to the Colorado State Patrol.

The truck, which was hauling an empty trailer, crossed a bridge before losing control and rolling. The trailer came to rest on a concrete embankment below the bridge. The 58-year-old driver was not injured in the crash.

The left and middle lanes of eastbound I-70 were closed for the crash investigation and vehicle recovery. Colorado Department of Transportation cameras showed traffic backed up to the Sixth Avenue exit during the closure.

stories.headline: Denver sports teams plan 500 acres of stadium-anchored mixed-use development
stories.summary:
Denver's professional sports franchises are advancing plans to develop approximately 500 acres of stadium-anchored mixed-use projects across the metro area, according to the Denver Business Journal. The plans involve Kroenke Sports and Entertainment and the Denver Broncos organization and were discussed at a summit focused on the future of sports-adjacent urban development.

The proposed developments would be anchored by existing or planned sports venues and could include housing, retail, office and entertainment components. Details on timelines, financing and specific parcels involved were not fully disclosed in available reporting.

Such stadium-anchored developments have become an increasingly common strategy for sports franchise owners to generate revenue and drive neighborhood revitalization. If realized at the scale discussed, the projects would represent one of the most significant real estate undertakings in Denver's recent history.

stories.headline: Lakewood LASIK clinic ordered to pay over $7 million in malpractice case involving injured commercial pilot
stories.summary:
A Jefferson County District Court judge has entered a final judgment of more than $7.3 million against LASIKPlus, a laser eye surgery company operating in Lakewood, following a jury verdict in favor of a 27-year-old commercial pilot who was permanently injured during a 2023 procedure. Nicholas Lara alleged that LASIKPlus performed LASIK surgery on him despite pre-operative test results showing clear signs of keratoconus, a corneal condition that makes the surgery unsuitable, and without informing him of the risk.

The jury found LASIKPlus negligent and responsible for 75 percent of Lara's injuries after a seven-day trial in February. The court separately granted Lara's motion to exceed Colorado's $1 million statutory cap on damages under the Health Care Availability Act, finding that applying the cap would be unfair given Lara's young age, the permanent nature of his injury and the substantial evidence of lost earning capacity as a pilot.

The final judgment includes $5,797,500 in damages against LASIKPlus — comprised of $5,572,500 in economic damages and $225,000 in noneconomic damages — plus more than $1.5 million in prejudgment interest, for a total exceeding $7.3 million. Lara's attorneys called the case a matter of patient safety, arguing that LASIKPlus placed profit above proper screening protocols.

stories.headline: Colorado marijuana company recalls pesticide-contaminated flower sold at metro Denver dispensaries
stories.summary:
A Colorado marijuana producer has issued a voluntary recall of flower products sold at dispensaries across the Denver metro area after state testing detected pesticide levels exceeding legal limits. The Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment and the Department of Revenue announced Thursday that batches produced by Levels IV were found to contain the pesticide chlorfenapyr.

Affected products were sold between September 9 and March 26 at dispensaries in Denver, Aurora, Northglenn, Broomfield and Colorado Springs. Denver's Spark Dispensary, Aurora's Jars Cannabis, Broomfield's The Dab, Northglenn's Doc's Apothecary and Colorado Springs' Bleezies are among the named retailers. Consumers can identify recalled batches by the retail cultivation license number 403R-00235 and batch labels ROOM 2-A 6/17 and ROOM 2-B 6.17.

The recall includes batches that initially passed testing but were flagged during a subsequent investigation into laboratory testing concerns. State officials advise consumers to destroy the product or return it to the point of purchase for disposal, and to seek immediate medical attention and report any adverse reactions to the Marijuana Enforcement Division.

stories.headline: Lionel Messi, Inter Miami set to face Colorado Rapids at Empower Field in historic match
stories.summary:
The Colorado Rapids are set to host Lionel Messi and Inter Miami at Empower Field at Mile High on Saturday afternoon in what is expected to be Messi's first-ever appearance in Colorado. The match, billed as the Rapids' 30th Anniversary game, has drawn enormous interest, with 60,000 tickets already sold as of last week and the potential to set a single-game attendance record for the club.

Messi is expected to play, having started and played the full 90 minutes in each of Miami's last four matches. His appearance in training footage this week and Inter Miami's lighter schedule following elimination from the CONCACAF Champions Cup have raised expectations that the eight-time Ballon d'Or winner will take the pitch. The game is being featured as the exclusive MLS broadcast on Apple TV during its time slot.

The Rapids enter the match in strong form under first-year coach Matt Wells, sitting sixth in the Western Conference and tied for the most goals scored in MLS this season with 19 through seven matches. Their most recent result was a 6-2 win over Houston. Kickoff is scheduled for 2:30 p.m. Mountain Time, with temperatures expected to reach approximately 55 degrees by then under clearing skies following Friday's snowstorm.

stories.headline: Dodgers rout Rockies 7-1 at snow-cleared Coors Field; Muncy hits two home runs
stories.summary:
The Los Angeles Dodgers defeated the Colorado Rockies 7-1 Friday night at Coors Field in Denver, with Max Muncy hitting two home runs and Tyler Glasnow allowing just two hits in seven innings. The game was played in frigid conditions, with temperatures at 36 degrees at first pitch after grounds crew members spent time clearing approximately 3 inches of snow from the playing surface earlier in the day.

Muncy went 3-for-4 with a double and three RBIs, ending a 1-for-17 slump and recording his 21st career multi-homer game. Shohei Ohtani went 2-for-5 in his return to the Dodgers lineup, extending his career-best on-base streak to 49 games. Glasnow, who improved to 2-0, struck out seven and walked two in his longest outing of the season.

Colorado starter Tomoyuki Sugano allowed five runs on nine hits across four innings, struggling after a strong start to the season. The Rockies have now lost seven of their last eight games after opening 6-6. The series continues Saturday night at Coors Field with Emmet Sheehan facing Ryan Feltner.

stories.headline: Nuggets open first-round playoff series against Timberwolves on Saturday with key players available
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets begin their first-round NBA playoff series against the Minnesota Timberwolves on Saturday, with key players returning from minor injuries ahead of Game 1. Guard Tim Hardaway Jr., who missed Denver's regular-season finale due to left knee soreness, is available and expected to play. Forward Johnson, who sat out the Nuggets' final two regular-season games with an ankle issue, has also been cleared for Game 1.

The first-round series schedule has been set, with subsequent games to follow through a potential seven-game series extending into early May. Denver enters the playoffs as a strong contender in the Western Conference, led by Nikola Jokic, who collected end-of-season honors.

The Nuggets-Timberwolves matchup is a rematch of recent playoff meetings between the two Western Conference rivals. Denver will host Games 1, 2 and, if necessary, Games 5 and 7 at Ball Arena.

stories.headline: Denver restaurant owners, city auditor challenge report used to justify tipped wage cut proposal
stories.summary:
More than 55 Denver restaurant owners have signed on to oppose reducing the city's tipped minimum wage, pushing back against a February report commissioned by Denver Economic Development and Opportunity, Visit Denver and private restaurant financing company inKind. The report, authored by founders of the Snooze and Jax Fish House restaurant groups, cited Denver's tipped minimum wage of $16.27 per hour as a significant industry burden and helped prompt a City Council member to explore legislation to lower it.

The Denver Auditor's Office released its own analysis identifying three specific claims in the report it says are inaccurate, including the use of outdated Bureau of Labor Statistics data that overstated restaurant closures. The auditor said updated BLS data shows Denver had 910 full-service restaurants in early 2023 and 795 by late 2024 — a decline, but far less dramatic than the report depicted. City Council member Darrell Watson, who had indicated interest in drafting a wage-reduction bill, declined comment and said no such bill is currently before the council.

The debate over tipped wages is now possible because of House Bill 25-1208, signed by Gov. Jared Polis in June 2025 and effective January 1, 2026, which for the first time allows Colorado cities to adjust their own tip credit. Denver's overall minimum wage stands at $19.29 per hour in 2026, a 74 percent increase since 2019. Both restaurant owners and labor advocates are watching closely as the policy debate continues.

joke.joke: All I can say about life is 'Oh God, enjoy it.'

topStory.headline: Iran Reimoses Strait of Hormuz Restrictions, Fires on Tanker One Day After Declaring Waterway Open
topStory.summary:
Iran reversed course Saturday and reimposed military control over the Strait of Hormuz, just 24 hours after declaring the critical waterway open for commercial transit. The Iranian military's Khatam al-Anbiya joint command headquarters announced that "control of the Strait of Hormuz has returned to its previous state" and that the waterway is now under "strict management and control of the armed forces." Iranian Revolutionary Guard gunboats also fired on at least one tanker attempting to transit the strait, according to the British military's United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations Center, which reported the vessel and crew were safe. The incident occurred approximately 20 nautical miles northeast of Oman, and no radio warning was issued before the gunboats opened fire.

Iran's military and political officials tied the reversal directly to the continued U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports. "Until America allows full freedom of navigation for vessels traveling from Iran to destinations and vice versa, the situation in the Strait of Hormuz will remain under strict control," Iran's military spokesperson said. Ebrahim Azizi, head of Iran's National Security Commission, accused the United States of "untrustworthiness" and said the closure was a response to "the enemy's new duplicity," adding that Trump had attempted to create "the impression of an agreement" through public statements. President Trump, speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, said a deal with Iran "is going to happen" and flatly rejected any Iranian restrictions or tolls on the strait.

The Strait of Hormuz is the world's most critical oil shipping chokepoint, with approximately 20 percent of global oil supply passing through it. The waterway had been contested since the United States imposed a naval blockade on Iranian ports earlier in the week, with U.S. Central Command reporting that 21 ships had been turned back to Iran since the blockade began Monday. Iran's announcement Friday that the strait was open had triggered a sharp drop in oil prices, with U.S. crude falling 11.4 percent to $83.85 per barrel and international Brent crude sliding 9 percent to $90.38 per barrel before Saturday's reversal.

The situation now threatens to deepen a global energy crisis and raise the risk of renewed military conflict, even as diplomatic mediators have expressed confidence that a broader agreement remains within reach. A ceasefire between the United States and Iran is set to expire Wednesday, and Trump said Friday it may not be extended. Iran's Navy warned that "any breach of commitments by America will receive an appropriate response," while the Revolutionary Guard Corps said the strait's status would remain restricted "as long as the passage of vessels from Iran and to Iran is threatened."
